title: Diverse GBDT Rank Ensemble With OOF Calibration
summary: The best current CV scores are very close, suggesting diminishing returns from more single-model features.
rationale: The best current CV scores are very close, suggesting diminishing returns from more single-model features. Public tabular guidance supports combining LightGBM, CatBoost, and XGBoost because their categorical handling and regularization create complementary errors; ROC AUC also benefits from rank-preserving blend search rather than hard calibration alone.
implementation_hint: Generate OOF/test predictions from 3-5 seeds each of LightGBM, CatBoost native categoricals, and XGBoost hist with the strongest feature set. Optimize blend weights on OOF AUC using rank-averaged predictions and optionally calibrate with isotonic/Platt only if it improves OOF AUC. Keep a grouped-by-race/year validation view to detect leaderboard-overfit blends.
expected_effect: Likely small but practical gain, around 0.0005-0.002 AUC if model errors are not fully correlated.
risk: Can overfit OOF if too many seeds/weights are tuned on one split; CatBoost may be slower, and calibration can worsen AUC if it distorts ranking.
